attributes.description:
Optional grouping label, e.g. `research`.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: duration-ms
attributes.description:
How long the call took, in milliseconds. Omitted from the chip entirely when unset.

Type: `number | undefined`

attributes.name: icon
attributes.description:
Literal icon hint (e.g. an emoji) used when the `icon` slot is empty ŌĆö see the class doc's
icon-precedence note. Ignored once anything is assigned to `slot="icon"`.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: name
attributes.description:
The tool/function name, e.g. `web_search`.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: status
attributes.description:
The call's current lifecycle state ŌĆö drives the glyph, color, and
`status-text`. Invalid runtime values use the pending presentation.

Type: `ToolCallStatus`  
Default: `'pending'`

attributes.name: summary
attributes.description:
Short human-readable status text, e.g. `Searching webŌĆ”`.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

elements.name: lyra-tool-param-form
elements.description:
`<lyra-tool-param-form>` ŌĆö renders one form control per top-level property
of a JSON Schema object, for ad hoc tool invocation or approval-editing UIs
(e.g. "the agent wants to call `create_event(title, attendees, allDay)` ŌĆö
let the user tweak the arguments before running it").

**Scope limitation (intentional, not accidental):** this renderer only
understands a *flat* object schema ŌĆö every `properties` entry must be
`'string'`, `'number'`, `'integer'`, `'boolean'`, or a string `enum`;
primitive `const` is also enforced. Nested objects, arrays,
`oneOf`/`anyOf`/`allOf`, `$ref`, constraints such as `minLength`/`minimum`,
and schema-valued `additionalProperties` are not read. A full
JSON-Schema-to-form renderer is
out of scope for this component; a property whose `type` isn't one of the
four above renders a visible "Unsupported field type" note and marks the
form invalid instead of silently dropping it or throwing.

Fields render in `Object.keys(schema.properties)` order (insertion order,
which is reliable for a plain object's string keys). A field's label is
`title ?? ` the property key; `description` renders as helper text below
the control; a key listed in `required` gets a visible `*`. The outer
component owns validation because JSON Schema `required` means property
presence, unlike HTML controls' nonempty/must-check semantics.

This component owns no Submit/Cancel/Approve chrome ŌĆö a consumer composes
it inside their own dialog (e.g. a tool-approval dialog) and reads
`.value`/`.errors`/`checkValidity()` (or calls `reportValidity()` right
before acting, which also reveals any inline errors that user interaction
hasn't surfaced yet).

`value` is exactly what the consumer last set it to ŌĆö a field with no
entry in `value` but a schema `default` displays (and is *emitted*, via
`lyra-input`) as that default, but the `value` *property* itself is left
alone until the user actually edits that field. This mirrors an
uncontrolled `<input placeholder>` not writing to `.value`, and means the
very first `lyra-input` a consumer receives already carries every default
resolved, so round-tripping `e.detail.value` back into `.value` converges
after one edit. JSON Schema defines `default` as an annotation; this form
renderer deliberately materializes it before validation and submission,
so a valid default can satisfy `required` here.

Optional native `<form>` participation is implemented via `ElementInternals`
attached directly (this component's value is a whole object, not a plain
string, so the `FormAssociated` string-value mixin doesn't fit ŌĆö same
shape as `<lyra-combobox>`'s array-valued case). This is a nice-to-have
layered on top of the primary integration contract (`value` +
`lyra-input`/`lyra-validity-change`), not a requirement: a consumer that
never puts this inside a `<form>` loses nothing.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The outer wrapper around all fields. |
| `description` | A field's helper text, from `schema.description`. |
| `empty` | The message shown when `schema.properties` has no entries. |
| `error` | A field-level or form-level validation message. |
| `field` | One property's wrapper (label + control + description + error). |
| `label` | A field's label. |
| `unsupported` | The fallback note rendered in place of a control for a property whose `type` is outside this renderer's scope. |

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Type: `boolean`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: name
attributes.description:
Submission key for the optional native `<form>` participation, reflected synchronously.

Type: `string`

elements.name: lyra-tool-result-dialog
elements.description:
`<lyra-tool-result-dialog>` ŌĆö a full tool-call detail overlay: a status/
duration header plus a `body` slot where a consumer typically places a
`<lyra-tabs>` with Input/Preview/JSON/Raw panels. This component knows
nothing about what's inside that slot ŌĆö it only supplies the modal chrome
around it.

This is its own standalone overlay implementation (`role="dialog"`,
focus-trapped, Escape/backdrop-dismissible, scroll-locking) rather than
nesting a `<lyra-dialog>` in its shadow template ŌĆö see `<lyra-dialog>`'s
own header comment for why a new overlay component in this library
duplicates that pattern locally instead of composing the previous one:
slot-forwarding into a nested `<lyra-dialog>` would put a forwarding
`<slot>` where a light-DOM-scanning descendant (e.g. a slotted
`<lyra-tabs>`'s own `Array.from(this.children)` scan) expects real
projected content.

`maximized` toggles between a constrained modal size and a near-fullscreen
size within the same open dialog and open/close lifecycle ŌĆö unlike
`<lyra-widget>`'s fullscreen mode, there's no non-modal resting state to
return to, so no separate scroll-lock/focus-trap bookkeeping is needed for
the transition itself.

Stacking: opening more than one of these dialogs at once is supported --
Escape and the Tab focus trap only ever act on the topmost open instance,
so instances beneath it stay open and untouched until the one on top closes.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `body` | The dialog's main content ŌĆö typically a `<lyra-tabs>` with Input/Preview/JSON/Raw panels, entirely consumer-assembled. |
| `footer` | Optional action buttons, rendered in a bottom row. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `backdrop` | The full-viewport scrim behind the panel. |
| `body` | The wrapper around the `body` slot. |
| `close-button` | The built-in close button. |
| `duration` | The formatted `duration-ms` text. |
| `footer` | The wrapper around the `footer` slot. |
| `header` | The row containing the tool name, status, duration, and toggle/close buttons. |
| `header-actions` | The wrapper around the maximize and close buttons. |
| `maximize-button` | The built-in maximize/restore toggle button. |
| `panel` | The dialog panel itself (`role="dialog"` while open). |
| `status` | The status badge (icon + text). |
| `title` | The wrapper around the tool name, status, and duration. |
| `tool-name` | The `tool-name` text. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lyra-tool-result-dialog-overlay-color` (default: `var(--lyra-color-overlay)`) ŌĆö Backdrop color.
- `--lyra-tool-result-dialog-maximized-inset` ŌĆö Insets for the maximized panel.
- `--lyra-tool-result-dialog-spin` (default: `1s linear`) ŌĆö Running-status animation duration and timing.

elements.name: lyra-tool-result-view
elements.description:
`<lyra-tool-result-view>` ŌĆö renders a tool call's result via whichever
custom renderer a host app has registered for it (see `registerToolRenderer()`
in `registry.ts`), falling back to `<lyra-json-viewer>` whenever no
renderer matches, a candidate renderer's `matches()` predicate throws during
dispatch, a renderer's optional `load()` rejects, or its `render()` throws.
This component owns none of the actual visual weight of a
populated tool result ŌĆö that's entirely whatever the registered renderer
returns; this is just the dispatch + fallback + loading-state shell.

Dispatch runs against `registry` when set, otherwise against the
module-level default registry `registerToolRenderer()` writes to ŌĆö see
`findToolRenderer()`'s two-step (exact name, then shape-based `matches()`)
lookup order for the full rule.

`fallback` implements two kinds: `"json"` (the default, an unconditional `<lyra-json-viewer>`)
and `"text"`, which renders a *string* `result` as preformatted text instead ŌĆö falling back to
the `"json"` behavior when `result` isn't a string, so setting `fallback="text"` defensively
against an unpredictable result shape never renders broken output. `copyable` adds a
copy-to-clipboard affordance to either fallback kind (forwarded to `<lyra-json-viewer>`'s own
`copyable` for `"json"`; a `<lyra-copy-button>` alongside the text for `"text"`).

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root wrapper around the resolved renderer's output (or the loading/fallback view). |
| `fallback-copy` | The `<lyra-copy-button>` shown when `copyable` is set alongside the `fallback="text"` kind (only present when both are set). |
| `fallback-text` | The `<pre>` element for the `fallback="text"` kind's preformatted result text (only present in that mode). |

attributes.name: copyable
attributes.description:
Shows a copy-to-clipboard affordance alongside the fallback view (both `"json"` and `"text"` kinds) ŌĆö forwarded to `<lyra-json-viewer>`'s own `copyable`, or renders a `<lyra-copy-button>` next to the text fallback.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: fallback
attributes.description:
Fallback-kind selector ŌĆö see the class doc's `fallback` paragraph for the full "json" vs "text" behavior.

Type: `string`  
Default: `'json'`

elements.name: lyra-tool-select-dialog
elements.description:
`<lyra-tool-select-dialog>` ŌĆö a category-grouped, filterable, searchable
tool-enablement dialog for picking which agent tools are available in a
conversation.

This renders its own dialog panel rather than nesting a `<lyra-dialog>` in
its shadow template. Shared overlay infrastructure coordinates stacking,
focus trapping, Escape/backdrop dismissal, and focus return with every
other overlay in the same document.

`useDefaults` is a single top-level switch: while `true`, every per-tool
checkbox below renders disabled (still reflecting whatever `selected`
holds ŌĆö a consumer should populate that with its own default tool set
whenever `useDefaults` is true) and a hint explains that turning the
switch off is how to customize. Turning it off is the "customize"
affordance ŌĆö it's the only thing that both flips `useDefaults` to `false`
*and* unlocks the per-tool checkboxes for editing, so there's exactly one
control for that transition rather than a separate button duplicating it.

There is no built-in footer/close button ŌĆö like `<lyra-dialog>`, dismissal
happens via Escape, a backdrop click, or a consumer's own `footer`-slotted
action calling `close()`. This also means the search input is the very
first focusable element in the panel with no special-casing needed, so
it's what receives focus on open (see `updated()`).

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `footer` | Optional action buttons (e.g. a "Done" button), rendered in a bottom row. Changes already apply live via `lyra-change`, so this is optional. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `backdrop` | The full-viewport scrim behind the panel. |
| `body` | The scrollable wrapper around the grouped tool list. |
| `category` | A single category's wrapper (`role="group"`). |
| `category-count` | The terse, `aria-hidden` tool count next to a category heading (the heading's accessible name gets the full sentence from an sr-only sibling instead). |
| `category-heading` | A category's heading. |
| `category-list` | The `<ul>` of tool rows within a category. |
| `defaults-hint` | The "turn off to customize" hint, shown only while `useDefaults` is true. |
| `defaults-row` | The wrapper around the use-defaults switch and its hint. |
| `defaults-toggle` | The built-in `<lyra-switch>` bound to `useDefaults`. |
| `empty` | The "no tools" / "no matches" message. |
| `footer` | The wrapper around the `footer` slot. |
| `header` | The wrapper around the title/subtitle. |
| `panel` | The dialog panel itself (`role="dialog"` while open). |
| `search-input` | The filter text input. |
| `search-row` | The wrapper around the search input. |
| `subtitle` | The "N of M tools enabled" summary line. |
| `title` | The dialog's heading. |
| `tool-checkbox` | A row's `<lyra-checkbox>`. |
| `tool-description` | A row's optional description text. |
| `tool-disabled-reason` | A disabled row's `disabledReason` text, slotted inside `tool-checkbox` (alongside `tool-name`/`tool-description`) so it contributes to the checkbox's accessible name/description instead of going unannounced. |
| `tool-icon` | A row's leading icon glyph, when `icon` is set. |
| `tool-name` | A row's name text (plus its `icon`, if set). |
| `tool-row` | A single tool's `<li>` row. |

Type: `string | null`

elements.name: lyra-tour
elements.description:
`<lyra-tour>` -- a spotlight-and-step guided walkthrough for first-run onboarding. A sequence
of steps, each anchored to a target element elsewhere in the page via the shared Floating UI
positioner, shown against a dimmed full-viewport backdrop with a cutout/ring highlighting the
current target, with Next/Previous/Skip controls and a step-progress indicator. First-party
invention (no Web Awesome equivalent) -- nearest precedent in shape is `lyra-dialog` (overlay
lifecycle/focus trap) + `lyra-carousel` (index-based navigation) + `lyra-stepper`
(progress/RTL arrow-key nav).

**Not a form-associated control.** A tour is a walkthrough, not a field -- it deliberately has
no `label`/`hint`/`error` chrome and no `FormAssociated` mixin.

**Controlled component.** `steps` is never mutated by this component (mirrors
`lyra-stepper`'s `steps`); only `activeIndex` and `open` are self-managed, mirroring
`lyra-carousel`'s `index`.

**Target interactivity.** By default, the step's spotlighted target is non-interactive while
its step is active: it stays visually revealed and perceivable/announceable by assistive tech
(not `inert`, not `aria-hidden`) but cannot be clicked -- every pointer event over the full
viewport, including directly over the visually-revealed target, is captured by the backdrop
(CSS `mask` does not affect hit-testing, only `clip-path` does) -- and cannot be reached by Tab
(the shared overlay focus trap confines Tab to the popover panel). Set `step.interactiveTarget`
to opt a step's target out of this: the backdrop additionally clips itself (via `clip-path`,
which *does* affect hit-testing) around the same rect, so pointer/click events fall through to
the live target underneath. This only restores pointer/click reachability, not Tab
reachability -- the focus trap still confines keyboard focus to the step popover regardless of
`interactiveTarget`. If the interaction a step demonstrates must also be keyboard-reachable,
don't rely on Tab to reach the live target -- have the app's own listener on the target call
`tourEl.next()` to advance the tour programmatically.

**Focus management.** Uses the shared overlay manager with `modal: false` -- a deliberate
choice, not an oversight. The default `modal: true` would mark the entire rest of the page
(including the spotlighted target itself, since it lives outside `<lyra-tour>`'s own ancestor
chain) `inert`, stripping it from the accessibility tree entirely -- stronger than the
"non-interactive but still perceivable" default described above. `modal: false` skips that
DOM-wide `inert` marking; the shared Tab-trap still confines keyboard Tab to the popover panel
unconditionally regardless of `modal`, and the backdrop's own hit-testing handles
pointer/click reachability, so nothing is lost. `role="dialog"` and `aria-modal="true"` are
still rendered on the popover panel anyway: most screen readers restrict their own virtual-
cursor/browse-mode navigation to an `aria-modal="true"` element's subtree, giving assistive-
tech users the same "can perceive, can't wander into, the live target" experience that the
Tab-trap and backdrop hit-testing already give sighted/mouse users.

Each step transition mounts a genuinely new popover DOM node (keyed on the step's `id`) so
focus reliably re-enters the panel every time, even though the Previous/Next button that
triggered the transition lives inside that same persistent-looking region.

No `Home`/`End` jump-to-first/last-step shortcut and no click-to-jump progress dots, unlike
`lyra-stepper` -- a tour's steps are tied to live DOM targets that may not exist until an
earlier step's side effect (opening a menu, navigating a route) has run, so free jumping is
unsafe by default. `goToStep()` remains available for a host that knows what it's doing (e.g.
a "restart tour" affordance elsewhere).

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | Rich content overriding the currently active step's plain-text `content` for that step only. When real content is assigned, it's shown instead of `step.content`; when empty, `step.content` renders as plain text. Not scoped per step by this component itself -- a consumer that needs different rich content per step swaps the slotted children (or listens for `lyra-tour-step-change` and re-renders them) itself, the same "consumer owns slotted content" pattern `lyra-dialog`'s default slot already uses. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `backdrop` | The full-viewport dimmed scrim with the spotlight cutout, an inline `<svg>`. `aria-hidden="true"`. |
| `body` | Wrapper around the step's content (slotted or `step.content`). |
| `footer` | Wrapper around the Previous/Skip/Next-or-Done control row. |
| `heading` | The step's visible heading text element -- the `aria-labelledby` target. |
| `next-button` | The Next/Done control (label switches on the last step). |
| `popover` | The step panel itself. `role="dialog"`. |
| `previous-button` | The Previous control. |
| `progress` | Wrapper around the built-in step-progress indicator (dots + text). |
| `progress-dot` | An individual decorative dot within `progress`. `aria-hidden="true"`. |
| `progress-text` | The visible "Step X of Y" text -- one of the popover's `aria-describedby` targets. |
| `skip-button` | The Skip control. |
| `spotlight` | The decorative highlight ring drawn around the current target's (padded) rect. `pointer-events: none`, `aria-hidden="true"`. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lyra-tour-backdrop-color` ŌĆö Backdrop scrim fill. Defaults to `--lyra-color-overlay`.
- `--lyra-tour-spotlight-radius` ŌĆö Corner radius shared by the cutout and the ring. Defaults to `--lyra-radius`.
- `--lyra-tour-spotlight-ring-color` ŌĆö Spotlight ring color. Defaults to `--lyra-color-brand`.
- `--lyra-tour-spotlight-ring-width` ŌĆö Spotlight ring thickness. Defaults to `--lyra-border-width-medium`.
- `--lyra-tour-popover-max-width` ŌĆö Maximum popover inline size. Defaults to `--lyra-size-22rem`.

attributes.description:
Index of the currently active step, clamped to `[0, steps.length - 1]` by `goToStep()`.

Type: `number`  
Default: `0`

attributes.name: aria-label
attributes.description:
Host-level `aria-label` override for every step popover's accessible name -- wins over each
step's own `heading`, matching `lyra-dialog`'s `accessibleLabel` pattern. Most consumers
won't need this since each step already has a meaningful name via `heading`; setting it
makes the *same* string name every step's panel. Set as a plain `aria-label` attribute on

Distance (px) between the target and the popover, passed straight to Floating UI's
`offset()` middleware -- a tour-level-only setting, mirroring `lyra-popover`'s `distance`
prop exactly.

Type: `number`  
Default: `12`

attributes.name: light-dismiss

Whether a backdrop click dismisses the tour (`end('skip')`). Defaults to `false` -- a
deliberate inversion of `lyra-dialog`'s `noLightDismiss` (opt-out, default
dismiss-on-backdrop-click): a guided tour's backdrop click doing nothing by default avoids
losing onboarding progress to a stray click. Set this to restore dismiss-on-backdrop-click.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: open
attributes.description:
Whether the tour is open. Set this (or call `start()`/`end()`) -- there is no separate
`show()`/`hide()` pair.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: placement
attributes.description:
Tour-level default Floating UI placement, overridable per step via `TourStep.placement`.

Type: `Placement`  
Default: `'bottom'`

attributes.name: show-progress

Whether the built-in "Step X of Y" progress indicator (dots + text) renders in the footer.

Tour-level default extra px between a target's own box and the spotlight cutout/ring,

Type: `number`

elements.name: lyra-tree
elements.description:
`<lyra-tree>` ŌĆö an expand/collapse hierarchy for graph/document navigation.

Implements the WAI-ARIA treeitem keyboard pattern: a single roving
`tabindex` (tracked here as `activeId`, pushed down to every
`<lyra-tree-node>` ŌĆö including nested ones, recursively) and
ArrowUp/Down/Right/Left/Home/End/Enter/Space handled by one delegated
`keydown` listener. Native `KeyboardEvent`s are `composed: true` and
bubble across shadow-DOM boundaries, so a press inside a deeply-nested
`<lyra-tree-node>`'s own shadow root still reaches this listener.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | `<lyra-tree-node>` elements (top-level tree items). |

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |

| `base` | The tree's root wrapper (role="tree"). |
| `empty` | The empty-state message shown when `data` is empty. |

`<lyra-tree-node>` ŌĆö internal recursive renderer for `<lyra-tree>`.

`role="treeitem"` (plus `aria-expanded`/`aria-level`/`aria-setsize`/
`aria-posinset` and the roving `tabindex`, driven by `<lyra-tree>`) live on
the *host* element, not the internal `[part="row"]` div ŌĆö that makes this
node's own nested children (rendered in *its own* shadow root as further
`role="group"` content) genuine DOM descendants of the treeitem, which is
what the WAI-ARIA treeitem pattern requires (previously a shadow-root
sibling).

elements.name: lyra-typing-indicator
elements.description:
`<lyra-typing-indicator>` ŌĆö a purely presentational "assistant is
responding" presence cue. No events, no interactivity: a consumer mounts
it while a response is being generated and removes it (or hides it) once
real content has arrived.

Three visual variants share one component rather than three separate tags
because callers pick between them along a single axis ŌĆö how the
surrounding surface wants the cue to read ŌĆö and nothing else about the
component differs:
- `dots` (default) ŌĆö three dots with a staggered bounce, the classic
  "typingŌĆ”" affordance for a standalone status line.
- `pulse` ŌĆö a single breathing dot, a quieter cue for a tighter space
  (e.g. next to an avatar).
- `cursor` ŌĆö a blinking vertical bar, meant to sit inline at the tail end
  of streamed text that is still being appended to.

Accessibility: this indicator typically mounts and unmounts around a real
generation lifecycle (appears when a response starts, disappears once one
arrives) rather than emitting a stream of updates of its own, so it does
*not* route through `<lyra-live-region>`/`Announcer`
(`../../internal/announcer.js`) ŌĆö that machinery exists to coalesce many
rapidly-changing announcements into one, and there is only ever a single
announcement here: the mount itself. A plain `role="status"` plus an
accessible name derived from `label` is sufficient, set both as an
`aria-label` on the host *and* as a visually-hidden text node in the
shadow tree, so the name survives even if only one of the two is picked up
by a given assistive-tech/browser pairing. An untouched-default, empty, or
whitespace-only `label` falls back to the localized "ThinkingŌĆ”" copy in
both places, rather than leaving the component with no accessible name at
all. The animated dots/pulse/cursor
shape is `aria-hidden="true"` ŌĆö it's decorative; `label` is the entire
accessible content, so nothing narrates individual animation frames.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The decorative (`aria-hidden`) wrapper around the animated shape. |
| `cursor` | The blinking bar in the `cursor` variant. |
| `dot` | Each of the three dots in the `dots` variant. |
| `pulse` | The single pulsing dot in the `pulse` variant. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lyra-transition-ambient` (default: `1.8s ease-in-out`) ŌĆö Animation duration and timing function shared by all variants.
- `--lyra-typing-dot-stagger-1` (default: `600ms`) ŌĆö Delay for the second dot in the dots variant.
- `--lyra-typing-dot-stagger-2` (default: `1200ms`) ŌĆö Delay for the third dot in the dots variant.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Accessible name, exposed via `role="status"`. Not re-announced on every
animation frame ŌĆö only mount (and any later change to this property)
produces a new announcement. An untouched-default, empty, or
whitespace-only value falls back to the localized "ThinkingŌĆ”" copy (see
`accessibleLabel`) so the component never loses its accessible name.

Type: `string`  
Default: `'ThinkingŌĆ”'`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: size
attributes.description:
Compact sizing for dense layouts (e.g. inline with a message bubble).

Type: `TypingIndicatorSize`  
Default: `'md'`

attributes.name: variant
attributes.description:
Which decorative presentation to render.

Type: `TypingIndicatorVariant`  
Default: `'dots'`

elements.name: lyra-virtual-list
elements.description:
`<lyra-virtual-list>` ŌĆö a generic windowed/virtualized list host. Renders
only the items within the current viewport (plus `overscan` padding rows
on each side) as real DOM, regardless of how large `items` is, so a
multi-thousand-row chat history sidebar or long message thread stays cheap
to scroll.

Content is entirely caller-supplied: `renderItem(item, index)` returns
whatever `lit-html` value should represent that row (typically a
`TemplateResult`), and `keyFunction(item, index)` gives it a stable
identity for `repeat()`'s DOM-reconciliation key, so scroll position and
any per-row state (e.g. an `<audio>` element's playback position) survive
an `items` mutation instead of every row remounting from scratch.

**Windowing math.** Every row ŌĆö in both `row-height` modes ŌĆö is positioned
by a `transform: translateY(offset)` computed from a single cumulative
`offsets` array (`offsets[i]` = the pixel top of row `i`), rather than by
page flow. This is what lets only a small DOM window exist while the
scrollbar still reflects the *true* total content height:
- **`row-height="auto"` (default).** Each currently-rendered row is
  watched by a `ResizeObserver`; its real height lands in a per-key
  `Map`, and any row not yet measured contributes `DEFAULT_ROW_ESTIMATE_PX`
  to the offsets array until it has been. `offsets` is *not* the same
  thing as a page-count-based `padding-top`/`padding-bottom` spacer pair ŌĆö
  that approach reflows every unmeasured row's position on every new
  measurement, which is exactly what per-row transform offsets avoid: only
  the rows *after* a newly-measured one shift, and even that shift is a
  cheap style recompute, not a layout-affecting padding change.
- **Fixed numeric `row-height`.** No measurement needed ŌĆö `offsets[i]` is
  just `i * rowHeightPx`, computed by the exact same cumulative-array code
  path (a dedicated "single spacer + start-index" special case was
  considered and would work too, but reusing one code path for both modes
  avoids maintaining two parallel rendering strategies for a difference
  that's otherwise just "how is row *i*'s height looked up").

The `offsets` array is rebuilt only when `items`, `row-height`, or
`keyFunction` change, or a row's measured height changes -- not on every
update, so a pure scroll-position tick (potentially every rAF while
scrolling) only re-runs the cheap range/visibility math in
`computeRange()`, never the `O(n)` offsets rebuild (which, in
`row-height="auto"` mode, also means a `keyFunction` call per item). For
the list sizes this component is meant for (a scrollable history sidebar,
realistically hundreds to a few thousand rows) that rebuild is a trivial
`O(n)` arithmetic loop even when it does run; it is *not* the right
approach for a hundred-thousand-row list without further work (e.g. a
Fenwick/segment tree for `O(log n)` offset queries+updates), which is out
of scope here.

**Accessibility.** The scroll container is `role="list"` and each rendered
row is `role="listitem"`, deliberately *not* `listbox`/`option` ŌĆö this
component only provides windowing, not the roving-tabindex/
`aria-activedescendant` keyboard-interaction contract ARIA requires
alongside a real `listbox`. A consumer that wants full single-select
listbox semantics on top of this should compose that behavior itself (see
`<lyra-select>`'s pattern), the same way this component's `active-id`
only *scrolls* the matching row into view and marks it `aria-current` ŌĆö
it never claims to be a selection widget. `aria-setsize`/`aria-posinset`
are computed from the row's real index in the full `items` array (not its
position among the currently-rendered DOM window), so a screen reader
still announces e.g. "item 12 of 340" correctly. `[part="base"]` itself
carries `tabindex="0"` ŌĆö `renderItem`'s content is caller-supplied and not
guaranteed to contain a focusable element, and a scrollable region with no
focusable content of its own is otherwise unreachable by keyboard (native
arrow/Page Up/Page Down scrolling included).

**Grouping.** When supplied, `groups` renders a labeled group marker at the
corresponding `startIndex`. Group markers are positioned independently of
the row window, so they remain available when the first row in a group is
outside the current overscanned range.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The scrollable container (`role="list"`). |
| `group` | A positioned group label. |
| `row` | One rendered row's absolutely-positioned wrapper (`role="listitem"`); `renderItem`'s return value renders inside it. |
| `spacer` | The full-content-height inner element that gives the container its true scrollable extent. |

attributes.name: active-id
attributes.description:
When set and it matches a row's typed `keyFunction` result, that row is
smoothly scrolled into view whenever this changes. Attribute values are
strings; assign the property for a numeric key.

Type: `VirtualListKey | ''`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: has-more
attributes.description:
When true, scrolling near the bottom fires `lyra-load-more`.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: loading
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: overscan
attributes.description:
Extra rows rendered beyond the visible viewport on each side, to reduce
blank-frame risk during fast scrolling. Normalized to a whole number in
the inclusive range 0ŌĆō`MAX_OVERSCAN_ROWS`; non-finite values use the
default.

Default: `DEFAULT_OVERSCAN_ROWS`

attributes.name: row-height
attributes.description:
`'auto'` (default) measures each row's real height via `ResizeObserver`;
a numeric string fixes every row to that many pixels.

Type: `string`  
Default: `'auto'`

elements.name: lyra-widget
elements.description:
`<lyra-widget>` ŌĆö a titled panel shell with an optional collapse toggle and
an optional fullscreen-expand toggle. Fullscreen promotes the same host
element in place (a CSS state, not a clone/portal), so slotted content
(a chart, a running simulation, scroll position) survives the transition.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | The panel body. |
| `actions` | Header action controls, rendered before the collapse/expand buttons. |
| `collapse-icon` | Overrides the built-in chevron glyph inside the collapse/expand toggle button entirely, via the platform's own slot-fallback-content mechanism (same convention as `<lyra-tool-call-chip>`'s `icon` slot): whatever is assigned wins, otherwise the default chevron renders. Only meaningful while `collapsible`. |
| `fullscreen-icon` | Overrides the built-in expand/close glyph inside the fullscreen toggle button entirely, using the same mechanism -- the override replaces *both* the "expand" and "exit fullscreen" default icons, so a consumer supplying one is responsible for its own expand/exit distinction (e.g. by reading the `fullscreen` attribute). Only meaningful while `expandable`. |
| `icon` | Optional leading icon in the title row. |
| `label` | Rich label content (overrides the `label` attribute). |
| `sublabel` | Rich sublabel content (overrides the `sublabel` attribute). |
| `view-{id}` | Content for the view whose `WidgetView.id` matches `{id}`, rendered when `views` is non-empty. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `actions` | The wrapper around the `actions` slot. |
| `backdrop` | The fullscreen scrim behind the panel. `fullscreen-inset` overrides the default `var(--lyra-space-l)` inset applied to `[part="base"]` and `[part="backdrop"]` while fullscreen (e.g. `"0 0 0 240px"` to leave a persistent sidebar visible). `compact` tightens header/body padding ŌĆö same convention as `lyra-empty`'s `compact`. |
| `base` | The panel root (dialog role + backdrop when fullscreen). |
| `body` | The wrapper around the default slot (the panel body). |
| `collapse-button` | The collapse/expand toggle button. |
| `fullscreen-button` | The fullscreen toggle button. |
| `header` | The header row containing the title, actions, and toggle buttons. |
| `icon` | Wrapper around the `icon` slot. Hidden entirely when empty. |
| `label` | The panel title text. |
| `label-group` | Wrapper around the label and sublabel. |
| `sublabel` | The panel subtitle text. |
| `title` | The wrapper around the label/sublabel. |
| `view-toggle` | A single view toggle button. |
| `view-toggles` | The header toggle-button group, only rendered when `views` is non-empty. |

attributes.name: aria-label
attributes.description:
Overrides the fullscreen dialog's accessible name, taking precedence over both `label` and a
slotted `label`. Fed only by a host `aria-label`, matching `lyra-scroller`'s/`lyra-carousel`'s
own host-override pattern.

Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.name: backdrop-inset
attributes.description:
Overrides the fullscreen *backdrop*'s own inset independent of `fullscreen-inset` -- e.g.
`"0"` to dim the full viewport-to-panel-edge region while the panel itself keeps a narrower
`fullscreen-inset`. Unset (the default) falls back to `fullscreen-inset`, i.e. today's exact
coupled behavior.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: collapsible
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: compact
attributes.description:
Tighter header/body padding for constrained spaces.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: expandable
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: fullscreen
attributes.description:

attributes.name: fullscreen-inset
attributes.description:
Raw CSS `inset` shorthand applied to the fullscreen panel and backdrop instead of the default
`var(--lyra-space-l)` on every side ŌĆö e.g. `"0 0 0 240px"` to leave a 240px persistent sidebar
visible while fullscreen.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.description:

attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

elements.name: lyra-word-cloud
elements.description:
`<lyra-word-cloud>` ŌĆö a zero-dependency SVG word/tag cloud. First-party
invention (no Web Awesome equivalent). Each word's rendered size is scaled
from its `weight` and placed via an outward Archimedean-spiral search (the
standard word-cloud heuristic: heaviest words placed first, each one
spiraling out from the center until it clears every word already placed).

Unlike sibling `lyra-sparkline`/`lyra-heatmap` (one `role="img"` glyph
standing in for an aggregate value), the individual words here are the
meaningful interactive content ŌĆö but with up to `MAX_WORDS` of them, making
every single one its own tab stop would be a poor keyboard experience.
Instead, like `lyra-heatmap`'s cells, the whole `[part="svg"]` is one tab
stop with roving arrow-key focus (Home/End jump to the first/last word,
Enter/Space activates the focused one), a drawn `[part="focus-ring"]`, and
a visually-hidden `aria-live="polite"` status announcement. The SVG focus
target carries the group role and accessible name so the semantics follow
the element a keyboard user actually focuses.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The word-cloud wrapper. |
| `empty` | The empty-state message. |
| `focus-ring` | The keyboard focus ring. |
| `live-region` | The visually hidden announcement region. |
| `svg` | The word-cloud SVG. |
| `word` | A rendered word. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lyra-word-cloud-color-1` (default: `var(--lyra-color-brand)`) ŌĆö First entry of the default categorical palette.
- `--lyra-word-cloud-color-2` (default: `var(--lyra-color-success)`) ŌĆö Second entry of the default categorical palette.
- `--lyra-word-cloud-color-3` (default: `var(--lyra-color-warning)`) ŌĆö Third entry of the default categorical palette.
- `--lyra-word-cloud-color-4` (default: `var(--lyra-color-danger)`) ŌĆö Fourth entry of the default categorical palette.
- `--lyra-word-cloud-color-5` (default: `var(--lyra-color-chart-1)`) ŌĆö Fifth entry of the default categorical palette.
- `--lyra-word-cloud-color-6` (default: `var(--lyra-color-chart-2)`) ŌĆö Sixth entry of the default categorical palette.
- `--lyra-word-cloud-color-7` (default: `var(--lyra-color-chart-3)`) ŌĆö Seventh entry of the default categorical palette.
- `--lyra-word-cloud-color-8` (default: `var(--lyra-color-chart-4)`) ŌĆö Eighth entry of the default categorical palette.

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: max-font-size
attributes.description:
Font size, in px, for the highest-weight word.

Default: `DEFAULT_MAX_FONT_SIZE`

attributes.name: min-font-size
attributes.description:
Font size, in px, for the lowest-weight word.

Default: `DEFAULT_MIN_FONT_SIZE`

attributes.name: orientations
attributes.description:
`mixed` lets some words render rotated 90┬░ for denser packing.

Type: `'horizontal' | 'mixed'`  
Default: `'horizontal'`

attributes.name: scale
attributes.description:
`sqrt` compresses the weight->font-size mapping so one heavy word doesn't dwarf the rest.

Type: `'linear' | 'sqrt'`  
Default: `'linear'`

elements.name: lyra-zoomable-frame
elements.description:
`<lyra-zoomable-frame>` ŌĆö a scrollable frame for inspecting slotted or
image content at a bounded zoom level. Scrolling provides panning when the
content exceeds the frame; the controls and keyboard shortcuts change zoom.

Two public reset methods cover different needs: `resetZoom()` (also wired to the built-in
reset button and the `0` keyboard shortcut) returns zoom to 1 while intentionally preserving
the current pan/scroll position; `resetView()` additionally returns pan to the origin, for a
caller (e.g. `<lyra-lightbox>`) that wants a fully clean view, such as when swapping to new
content entirely.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | Content to inspect; when `src` is set, an image is rendered instead. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The frame wrapper. |
| `content` | The transformed content wrapper. |
| `controls` | Zoom controls. |
| `reset` | Reset-to-100-percent button. |
| `viewport` | The scrollable viewport. |
| `zoom-in` | Zoom-in button. |
| `zoom-out` | Zoom-out button. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lyra-zoomable-frame-min-block-size` ŌĆö Minimum viewport block size.

properties.description: **`<lyra-pdf-viewer>`** (default: `var(--lyra-size-24rem)`) ŌĆö Block size of the virtualized page list.

properties.name: --lyra-qr-code-background
properties.description: **`<lyra-qr-code>`** (default: `var(--lyra-color-surface)`) ŌĆö Light/background module color, including the quiet zone.

properties.name: --lyra-qr-code-fill
properties.description: **`<lyra-qr-code>`** (default: `var(--lyra-color-text)`) ŌĆö Dark/foreground module color.

properties.name: --lyra-random-content-animation-duration
properties.description: **`<lyra-random-content>`** (default: `300ms`) ŌĆö Duration of the entrance animation.

properties.name: --lyra-random-content-animation-easing
properties.description: **`<lyra-random-content>`** (default: `ease`) ŌĆö Easing function for the entrance animation.

properties.name: --lyra-random-content-animation-translate
properties.description: **`<lyra-random-content>`** (default: `var(--lyra-size-0-5em)`) ŌĆö Translation distance for directional animations.

properties.name: --lyra-rating-empty-color
properties.description: **`<lyra-rating>`** (default: `var(--lyra-color-border)`) ŌĆö Unfilled-star color.

properties.name: --lyra-rating-fill
properties.description: **`<lyra-rating>`** (default: `var(--lyra-color-warning)`) ŌĆö Filled-star color.

properties.name: --lyra-rating-size
properties.description: **`<lyra-rating>`** (default: `var(--lyra-font-size-xl)`) ŌĆö Star size.

properties.name: --lyra-scroller-control-size
properties.description: **`<lyra-scroller>`** ŌĆö Control size.

properties.name: --lyra-scroller-min-block-size
properties.description: **`<lyra-scroller>`** ŌĆö Minimum vertical scroller size.

properties.name: --lyra-sparkline-area-opacity
properties.description: **`<lyra-sparkline>`** (default: `0.15`) ŌĆö Fill opacity of the area under the line (type="area").

properties.name: --lyra-sparkline-stroke-width
properties.description: **`<lyra-sparkline>`** (default: `1.5`) ŌĆö Stroke width of the line/area path.

properties.name: --lyra-svg-viewer-max-height
properties.description: **`<lyra-svg-viewer>`** (default: `none`) ŌĆö Maximum block size of the scrollable body before it scrolls internally. Also settable via the `max-height` property.

properties.name: --lyra-swatch-picker-selected-color
properties.description: **`<lyra-swatch-picker>`** (default: `var(--lyra-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Ring color drawn around the selected swatch, themeable independently of the focus ring and every other ring color.

properties.name: --lyra-textarea-max-block-size
properties.description: **`<lyra-textarea>`** (default: `none`) ŌĆö Maximum auto-grown block size before the textarea scrolls.

properties.name: --lyra-timeline-gap
properties.description: **`<lyra-timeline>`** (default: `var(--lyra-space-l)`) ŌĆö Spacing between consecutive items along the timeline's main axis; also the length each item's own rail visually bridges to reach the next item's marker. Declared here but actually consumed inside each `<lyra-timeline-item>`'s own stylesheet, via ordinary CSS custom-property inheritance across the slot boundary.

properties.name: --lyra-timeline-marker-color
properties.description: **`<lyra-timeline-item>`** (default: `var(--lyra-color-text-quiet)`) ŌĆö Marker fill/accent color. Swapped per `variant` (see the class doc's variant table); a consumer overriding this directly on one item wins over the variant default via normal CSS cascade/specificity.

properties.name: --lyra-timeline-marker-size
properties.description: **`<lyra-timeline-item>`** (default: `var(--lyra-size-1-25rem)`) ŌĆö Diameter of the marker circle (both inline-size and block-size, so the default dot stays circular).

properties.name: --lyra-timeline-rail-color
properties.description: **`<lyra-timeline-item>`** (default: `var(--lyra-color-border)`) ŌĆö Color of the connecting rail line. A component-scoped property (not just inlining `var(--lyra-color-border)` at every use site) so a consumer can retint just the rail without touching the library-wide border color elsewhere.

properties.name: --lyra-timeline-rail-width
properties.description: **`<lyra-timeline-item>`** (default: `var(--lyra-border-width-medium)`) ŌĆö Thickness of the connecting rail line.

properties.name: --lyra-tool-call-chip-spin
properties.description: **`<lyra-tool-call-chip>`** (default: `1s linear`) ŌĆö Running-icon animation duration and timing.

properties.name: --lyra-tool-result-dialog-maximized-inset
properties.description: **`<lyra-tool-result-dialog>`** ŌĆö Insets for the maximized panel.

properties.name: --lyra-tool-result-dialog-overlay-color
properties.description: **`<lyra-tool-result-dialog>`** (default: `var(--lyra-color-overlay)`) ŌĆö Backdrop color.

properties.name: --lyra-tool-result-dialog-spin
properties.description: **`<lyra-tool-result-dialog>`** (default: `1s linear`) ŌĆö Running-status animation duration and timing.

properties.name: --lyra-tooltip-background
properties.description: **`<lyra-tooltip>`** ŌĆö Tooltip background color (default `--lyra-color-neutral`).

properties.name: --lyra-tooltip-color
properties.description: **`<lyra-tooltip>`** ŌĆö Tooltip text color (default `--lyra-color-on-neutral`).

properties.name: --lyra-tooltip-max-inline-size
properties.description: **`<lyra-tooltip>`** ŌĆö Maximum inline size of the tooltip (default `--lyra-size-20rem`).

properties.name: --lyra-tour-backdrop-color
properties.description: **`<lyra-tour>`** ŌĆö Backdrop scrim fill. Defaults to `--lyra-color-overlay`.

properties.name: --lyra-tour-popover-max-width
properties.description: **`<lyra-tour>`** ŌĆö Maximum popover inline size. Defaults to `--lyra-size-22rem`.

properties.name: --lyra-tour-spotlight-radius
properties.description: **`<lyra-tour>`** ŌĆö Corner radius shared by the cutout and the ring. Defaults to `--lyra-radius`.

properties.name: --lyra-tour-spotlight-ring-color
properties.description: **`<lyra-tour>`** ŌĆö Spotlight ring color. Defaults to `--lyra-color-brand`.

properties.name: --lyra-tour-spotlight-ring-width
properties.description: **`<lyra-tour>`** ŌĆö Spotlight ring thickness. Defaults to `--lyra-border-width-medium`.

properties.name: --lyra-transition-ambient
properties.description:
**`<lyra-chat-message>`** (default: `1.8s ease-in-out`) ŌĆö Streaming-indicator animation duration and timing function.

**`<lyra-skeleton>`** (default: `1.8s ease-in-out`) ŌĆö Animation duration and timing function shared by the pulse and sheen effects.

**`<lyra-tool-call-chip>`** (default: `1.8s ease-in-out`) ŌĆö Pending-icon pulse duration and timing.

**`<lyra-typing-indicator>`** (default: `1.8s ease-in-out`) ŌĆö Animation duration and timing function shared by all variants.

properties.name: --lyra-typing-dot-stagger-1
properties.description: **`<lyra-typing-indicator>`** (default: `600ms`) ŌĆö Delay for the second dot in the dots variant.

properties.name: --lyra-typing-dot-stagger-2

properties.name: --lyra-word-cloud-color-1
